title: link
text: lnk_barrier_overrides(conn, barriers, observations, habitat, params, ) — processes fish observations habitat confirmations barrier skip list fresh. Counts observations upstream barrier via fwa_upstream() SQL, applies per-species thresholds, unions habitat confirmations. Output: (blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, species_code) table fresh skips access gating.

title: link
text: lnk_source(conn, crossings, label_col, label_map) — returns list(table, label_col, label_map) plugs directly frs_habitat(break_sources = list(...)). label_map translates link severity → fresh access labels (high → blocked, moderate → potential). lnk_aggregate(conn, crossings, habitat, cols_sum) — per-crossing upstream habitat rollup fresh output. Sums spawning_km, rearing_km (custom metrics).

Mismatch causes white space bands. Consistent element--frame spacing — inset elements visually equal margins frame edge Map fills frame — basemap extends edge--edge, dead bands. title: link
text: Behavioral guidelines reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Merge project-specific instructions needed. Tradeoff: guidelines bias toward caution speed. trivial tasks, use judgment.

title: link
text: Don’t assume. Don’t hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs. implementing: - State assumptions explicitly. uncertain, ask. - multiple interpretations exist, present - don’t pick silently. - simpler approach exists, say . Push back warranted. - something unclear, stop. Name ’s confusing. Ask.

title: link
text: Minimum code solves problem. Nothing speculative. features beyond asked. abstractions single-use code. “flexibility” “configurability” wasn’t requested. error handling impossible scenarios. write 200 lines 50, rewrite . Ask : “senior engineer say overcomplicated?” yes, simplify.

title: link
text: Touch must. Clean mess. editing existing code: - Don’t “improve” adjacent code, comments, formatting. - Don’t refactor things aren’t broken. - Match existing style, even ’d differently. - notice unrelated dead code, mention - don’t delete . changes create orphans: - Remove imports/variables/functions changes made unused. - Don’t remove pre-existing dead code unless asked. test: Every changed line trace directly user’s request.

title: link
text: Define success criteria. Loop verified. Transform tasks verifiable goals: - “Add validation” → “Write tests invalid inputs, make pass” - “Fix bug” → “Write test reproduces , make pass” - “Refactor X” → “Ensure tests pass ” multi-step tasks, state brief plan: Strong success criteria let loop independently. Weak criteria (“make work”) require constant clarification. guidelines working : fewer unnecessary changes diffs, fewer rewrites due overcomplication, clarifying questions come implementation rather mistakes.

MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. Query citation keys : SELECT idv.value items JOIN itemData id .itemID = id.itemID JOIN itemDataValues idv id.valueID = idv.valueID JOIN fields f id.fieldID = f.fieldID f.fieldName = 'citationKey'.

title: Scoring crossings for fish passage prioritization
text: fresh generic stream network engine. segments streams break points, classifies habitat species thresholds, rolls results. fresh doesn’t know crossings, severity, field assessments — ’s link’s job. link reads raw crossing data, applies field corrections, scores severity, produces break_sources spec plugs directly frs_habitat(). title: Compute upstream habitat per crossing — lnk_aggregate
text: half prioritization: severity alone tell invest. high-severity barrier 50m upstream habitat lower priority moderate barrier 15km spawning habitat. Flexible aggregation: cols_sum lets sum habitat metric — just spawning/rearing. Lake rearing hectares, wetland area, total accessible length — whatever habitat classification produced. Data flow:   link scores crossings. fresh segments network classifies habitat. link reads fresh's output compute per-crossing rollups.
code: link (score crossings) -> fresh (segment, classify) -> link (rollup)

title: Compute upstream habitat per crossing — lnk_aggregate
code: # --- "Two barriers are both high severity. #      One blocks 0.3km of rearing habitat. #      The other blocks 12km of spawning habitat for chinook. #      Which do you fix first?" --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Score crossings lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  # Compute upstream habitat from fresh output lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat") # Added spawning_km, rearing_km to working.crossings # Summary: #   spawning_km: min=0.0, median=2.3, max=45.1 #   rearing_km:  min=0.0, median=5.7, max=89.2  # Now you have severity + habitat — the full picture # ORDER BY severity DESC, spawning_km DESC # to find high-severity barriers blocking the most habitat.  # --- Custom metrics --- lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat",   cols_sum = c(spawning_km = "spawning",                rearing_km = "rearing",                lake_ha = "lake_rearing")) } # }

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: Override CSVs represent hand-reviewed crossing corrections accumulated across field seasons imagery review. row says "crossing's attribute changed value." Fail fast: structure validated data written. Missing required columns produce clear error naming column file path. code: # --- What does an override CSV look like? --- csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "overrides_example.csv", package = "link") overrides <- read.csv(csv_path) print(overrides) #>   modelled_crossing_id barrier_result_code  reviewer review_date         source #> 1                 1001            PASSABLE  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review #> 2                 1002             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review #> 3                 1003                NONE A. Irvine  2025-09-20    field visit #> 4                 1004            PASSABLE A. Irvine  2025-09-20    field visit #> 5                 1005             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-10-01 imagery review #   modelled_crossing_id barrier_result_code  reviewer review_date        source # 1                 1001            PASSABLE  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review # 2                 1002             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review # 3                 1003                NONE A. Irvine  2025-09-20   field visit # ... # Each row corrects one crossing. The reviewer and date tell you # who made the call and when — your audit trail.  # --- Load into database (the typical workflow) --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Single file — most common case lnk_load(conn,   csv  = "data/overrides/modelled_xings_fixes.csv",   to   = "working.overrides_modelled",   cols_required = c("barrier_result_code"))  # Multiple files from different field seasons lnk_load(conn,   csv  = c("data/overrides/2024_field.csv",            "data/overrides/2025_field.csv"),   to   = "working.overrides_modelled")  # Then validate and apply: lnk_override_validate(conn, "working.overrides_modelled",   "working.crossings") lnk_override_apply(conn, "working.crossings",   "working.overrides_modelled") } # }

title: Produce a fresh-compatible break source list — lnk_source

title: Produce a fresh-compatible break source list — lnk_source
code: # --- The link -> fresh handoff --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Step 1: Score crossings with link lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  # Step 2: Produce break source src <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings") # Returns: # list(table = "working.crossings", #      label_col = "severity", #      label_map = c(high = "blocked", moderate = "potential"))  # Step 3: Feed to fresh — link's output is fresh's input frs_habitat(conn, "BULK", break_sources = list(src))  # --- Combine with other break sources --- frs_habitat(conn, "BULK", break_sources = list(   src,   list(table = "working.falls", label = "blocked"),   list(table = "working.dams", label = "blocked"))) # link scored crossings + falls + dams — all as break sources.  # --- Custom label_map for a conservative project --- # Only treat high-severity as blocked src_strict <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings",   label_map = c(high = "blocked"))  # --- Static label for all crossings --- src_all <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings",   label = "potential", label_col = NULL) # Every crossing is a potential barrier — no severity differentiation. } # }

what: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: Build named list severity thresholds crossing scoring. Ships sensible BC fish passage defaults. Override via CSV inline arguments project-specific tuning.

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
code: lnk_thresholds(csv = NULL, high = NULL, moderate = NULL, low = NULL)

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: csv Path CSV file columns severity, metric, value. provided, CSV values override -code defaults. Use system.file("extdata", "thresholds_default.csv", package = "link") see expected format. high Named list metric thresholds high severity. Merged CSV values (inline wins conflict). moderate Named list metric thresholds moderate severity. low Named list metric thresholds low severity.

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: named list elements high, moderate, low, containing named list metric thresholds (numeric values keyed metric name).

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: Thresholds control lnk_score_severity() classifies crossings. default values reflect BC provincial fish passage assessment criteria: High severity outlet_drop >= 0.6m slope_length >= 120 — impassable species flows Moderate severity outlet_drop >= 0.3m slope_length >= 60 — flow-dependent, potentially passable migration flows Low severity everything else crossing present System-agnostic: metric names user-defined strings, hardcoded provincial data system. New Zealand user might define high = list(perch_height = 0.5, pipe_gradient = 0.05). Merge order: code defaults < CSV values < inline arguments. lets ship project CSV still tweak one threshold inline.

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
code: # Default BC thresholds — zero config th <- lnk_thresholds() th$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.6 # [1] 0.6  # Override from bundled CSV (same result, shows the format) csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "thresholds_default.csv", package = "link") th_csv <- lnk_thresholds(csv = csv_path) identical(th, th_csv) #> [1] TRUE  # Project-specific: bull trout tolerate higher drops th_bt <- lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8)) th_bt$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.8 # [1] 0.8 — inline override wins  # How thresholds plug into scoring (the integration point) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn() lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings",   thresholds = lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8))) } # }
